title: Would you try the new couch-style seating on United? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines plans to introduce Relax Row couch-style seating in 2027, transforming three economy seats into a couch.
A poll showed 61% of respondents are eager to try Relax Row, 24% are unsure, and 15% would not try it.
Readers expressed concerns about space, privacy, and costs, with some suggesting it might be more noise-prone and expensive.

title: This first grade teacher is running the Boston Marathon to support her students
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Siobhan Salani, a first grade teacher from Weymouth, is running the Boston Marathon for the Corey C. Griffin Foundation.
Salani teaches at Saint John Paul II Catholic Academy Lower Mills, where the foundation significantly benefits students.
The Corey C. Griffin Foundation provides unique opportunities to Boston children, reinforcing their mission of excellence.

title: A Maine beach is among the best in the U.S. in 2026, according to Conde Nast Traveler
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Sand Beach at Acadia National Park, Maine, is featured in Conde Nast Traveler's 2026 best U.S. beaches list.
Sand Beach is described as a hidden spot with pale sand between granite cliffs on Mount Desert Island.
Accommodations near Sand Beach include Under Canvas Acadia and The Asticou Hotel, noted by Vogue and Thrillist.

title: 16-year-old arrested in Allston shooting
author: John Waller
bullets: A 16-year-old boy was arrested for a shooting in Allston that injured another juvenile on Brighton Avenue.
The suspect faces multiple charges including assault with intent to murder and carrying a loaded firearm without a license.
The shooting occurred near 222 Brighton Ave. The victim's injuries were not life-threatening and they were hospitalized.

title: Alex Cora calls Terry Francona a ‘Hall of Fame manager’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora praised former manager Terry Francona as a 'Hall of Fame manager' before their Opening Day match.
Terry Francona, 2004-2011 Red Sox manager, now leads the Cincinnati Reds in his second season.
The Red Sox lost two of three games to the Reds in their 2026 season opener series.

title: Victim tied up by armed intruder during Beverly home invasion, police say
author: John Waller
bullets: An armed intruder broke into a Beverly home, tied up the occupant, and stole items including a vehicle.
The incident occurred on Saturday morning, with police alerted by a neighbor's 911 call at 8:50 a.m.
The victim was hospitalized for non-life-threatening injuries; the stolen vehicle was later recovered.

title: After ‘No Kings’ rally, local organizers are looking to sustain momentum. What’s next?
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The 'No Kings' rally drew over 180,000 people to Boston Common, emphasizing sustained engagement post-event.
Organizers encourage weekly actions like contacting officials and joining efforts to maintain momentum from the rally.
Plans for May Day include urging economic protest actions like skipping work or school and avoiding spending money.

title: Skier dies after fall at Sugarbush
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A skier died after sliding off a trail at Sugarbush Resort, Vermont, on Lincoln Peak's Stein's Run.
The man was found unresponsive by ski patrol and pronounced dead by the Mad River Valley Ambulance.
Authorities say the death does not appear suspicious; an autopsy will be conducted in Burlington.

title: As playoffs near, doubt the 2025-26 Bruins at your own risk
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins secured a 4-3 shootout win against the Columbus Blue Jackets with critical plays from Pavel Zacha and Viktor Arvidsson.
The Bruins have won several key games recently, elevating their playoff odds to nearly 92% according to MoneyPuck analytics.
Team president Cam Neely praised the Bruins' toughness and resilience, noting their ability to challenge top NHL teams this season.

title: Dating back to the 1600s, the second-oldest home in Cambridge lists for $5.8M
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The Hooper-Lee-Nichols House in Cambridge, built circa 1685, is listed for sale at $5.8 million.
Owned by History Cambridge, the property has served as the organization's headquarters for 70 years.
The 6,449-square-foot Georgian home features three stories, historic interiors, and preservation restrictions by Massachusetts Historical Commission.
